On 8 October 2019, a statue of Ibrahimović was unveiled in his hometown, outside Malmö's Stadion. The statue, created by Peter Linde, is 2.7 m (8 ft 9 in) tall and weighs almost 500 kg (1,100 lb).[442]

Religious and social background

Ibrahimović was born to a Bosniak Muslim father, Šefik Ibrahimović, and a Croat Catholic mother, Jurka Gravić; his parents were immigrants to Sweden from SFR Yugoslavia—Šefik from Bijeljina, SR Bosnia and Herzegovina and Jurka from Prkos [hr] near Škabrnja, SR Croatia—and met for the first time in Sweden.[443] Ibrahimović's parents divorced when he was a child, and although his upbringing was divided between them, he spent more time with his father: "I had time with my mother but I really lived with my father."[444][445] Relatives on both sides of his family were killed in the Bosnian War of the early 1990s.[446] He has said in a 2012 interview with PSG that: "My father is Muslim, my mother is Catholic, but none of that has anything to do with football. I received a special education. I'm me and football is a religion in its own right, and everyone is welcome..."[447] Ibrahimović was incensed in 2005 when Italian media attempted to build interest in a Juventus–Inter match by describing it as a "mini Balkan War" between himself and Serbian opponent Siniša Mihajlović. He said that he had no interest in nationalism, partially due to his mixed Bosniak-Croat parentage and his younger half-brother being fathered by a Serb.[448]

In an interview with CNN on 24 November 2015, he stressed that his Muslim background was "not a factor" in fans' perception of him: "For me, it didn't change (anything) because my father is Muslim and my mother is Catholic... For me it is all about respect. That's how I grew up and the way I learned to be. This is what I am."[449] Nevertheless he later described himself as a "deeply faithful Catholic".[16] The Malmö-based television presenter Teddy Landén, who was interviewed for the same documentary, noted Ibrahimović's choice of shirt name following his 2001 transfer to Ajax. He believed that the player changed from having Zlatan to having Ibrahimović on his jersey to honour his background and show to children from Rosengård and Malmö in general: 'If I can do it, you can do it.'"[450] In Ibrahimović's autobiography, he says that this change was only because his relationship with his father had improved at the time.[451]

On 18 February 2021, during a Europa League Round of 32 match between Red Star Belgrade and Milan at the Rajko Mitić Stadium, Ibrahimović was subjected to racism by the Red Star fans. Ibrahimović, who did not play the match, watched the game from the stands and jumped to celebrate once Milan scored, when a fan shouted insults at him, including "balija"—an ethnic slur targeting Bosnian Muslims. The match was played behind the closed doors due to the COVID-19 pandemic, but a few supporters were allowed to spectate from a VIP box. Red Star Belgrade issued an apology and condemned the insults; however, UEFA opened an investigation of the incident nevertheless.[452][453] On 14 April 2021, the club was fined €30,000 and ordered to play a home game in European competition behind closed doors.[454]

Tattoos

His surname is tattooed in Arabic on the back of his right arm, the names of his sons Vincent and Maximilian on his right arm. He has the common Buddhist "Five Deva Faces Yantra" representing wind, water, fire, creative perception and space, arranged in a way believed to ward off illness and injury, on his lower back, in addition to a roaring lion.[455] Another Buddhist tattoo, a "Yant Prajao Khao Nirote" is a protective emblem intended to end the suffering of a family, is on his upper right arm. The phrase "Only God can judge me" is tattooed on his ribcage. He also has a feather, a Koi fish, Polynesian tribal, and ace of hearts and clubs.[456]

  • UEFA Europa League: 2016–17
Individual
  • Ballon d'Or Nominations: 2003, 2004, 2005, 2007, 2008, 2009, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016[460]
  • Ballon d'Or 4th place: 2013
  • Golden Foot: 2012[461]
  • Globe Soccer Awards Player Career Award: 2022[462]
  • FIFA Puskás Award: 2013[463]
  • FIFA FIFPro World XI: 2013[464]
  • UEFA Team of the Year: 2007, 2009, 2013, 2014[465]
  • UEFA Ultimate Team of the Year (published 2015)[466]
  • UEFA European Championship Team of the Tournament: 2012[276]
  • UEFA Champions League Squad of the Season: 2013–14[467]
  • UEFA Europa League Squad of the Season: 2016–17[468]
  • ESM Team of the Year: 2006–07, 2007–08, 2012–13, 2013–14[469]
  • L'Équipe Journalists' Best XI: 2008[470]
  • UEFA Euro 2004: Goal of the Tournament: Italy vs Sweden[471]
  • UEFA Euro 2012: Goal of the Tournament: Sweden vs France[472]
  • UEFA Champions League top assist provider: 2012–13[473]
  • Eurosport Goal of the Year: 2004[474]
  • Club van 100
  • Juventus Player of the Year: 2004–05[475]
  • Serie A Player of the Month: September 2007, December 2008, January 2012, October 2020[476]
  • Serie A Foreign Footballer of the Year: 2004–05, 2007–08, 2008–09, 2010–11, 2011–12[477]
  • Serie A Footballer of the Year: 2007–08, 2008–09, 2010–11[478]
  • Serie A Most Loved Player: 2005[479]
  • Serie A Team of the Year: 2010–11, 2011–12[480][481]
  • Serie A Goal of the Year: 2008[479]
  • Capocannoniere: 2008–09, 2011–12[51][482]
  • Supercoppa Italiana Man of the Match: 2011
  • Gazzetta Sports Awards Legend: 2020[483]
  • Italy Best Foreign Athlete: 2021[484]
  • AC Milan Hall of Fame[485]
  • Golden Tapir: 2008, 2011, 2012, 2020, 2021, 2024[486]
  • Sportschau Goal of the Month Award: November 2012[487]
  • Sportschau Goal of the Year Award: 2012[487]
  • GQ Men of the Year: 2013[488]
  • SportAccord 'Play for Change' Award: 2015[489]
  • Ligue 1 Player of the Month: September 2012, January 2014, February 2014, March 2014, November 2015[490]
  • UNFP Just Fontaine Trophy Ligue 1 Best Forward of the Season: 2012–13[491]
  • Ligue 1 Best Foreign Player: 2012, 2013, 2014
  • Ligue 1 Player of the Year: 2012–13,[492] 2013–14,[493] 2015–16[492]
  • Ligue 1 Team of the Year: 2012–13, 2013–14, 2014–15, 2015–16[494][493][495][496]
  • Ligue 1 Top scorer: 2012–13, 2013–14, 2015–16[492]
  • Ligue 1 Goal of the Year: 2014[497]
  • Étoile d'Or France Football: 2014, 2016[498]
  • Trophée des Champions Man of the Match: 2014
  • Coupe de la Ligue Man of the Match: 2014–15[499]
  • Coupe de France Man of the Match: 2015–16[500]
  • Coupe de la Ligue Top scorer: 2014–15[501]
  • Coupe de France Top scorer: 2014–15, 2015–16[502][503]
  • Paris Saint-Germain Player of the Month: August 2012, September 2012, November 2013, January 2014, February 2015, November 2015, December 2015, January 2016, February 2016[504][505][506][507][508][509]
  • Paris Saint-Germain Hall of Fame[510]
  • Paris Saint-Germain Team of the History[511]
  • Medal of the City of Paris: 2016[512]
  • Eurosport European Player of the Month: August 2016[513]
  • Premier League Player of the Month: December 2016[514]
  • PFA Fans' Premier League Player of the Month: December 2016[515]
  • Manchester United Player of the Month: December 2016, February 2017[516]
  • Alan Hardaker Trophy: 2017[517]
  • EFL Cup top scorer: 2016–17[518]
  • MLS All-Star: 2018, 2019[519][520]
  • MLS Best XI: 2018, 2019[521][522]
  • MLS Newcomer of the Year: 2018[523]
  • MLS Goal of the Year: 2018[524]
  • MLS Greatest Goal: 2020[525]
  • Best MLS Player ESPY Award: 2019[526]
  • LA Galaxy Player of the Year: 2018, 2019[527][528]
  • LA Galaxy Golden Boot: 2018, 2019[527][528]
  • LA Galaxy Goal of the Year: 2018, 2019[529]
  • Pegasus Award: 2004[530]
  • Stor Grabb: 2004[531]
  • Guldbollen: 2005, 2007, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2020[532]
  • Jerringpriset: 2007[533]
  • Fotbollskanalens hederspris: 2008[534]
  • IFFHS Men's All Time Sweden Dream Team[535]
  • International Swede of the Year Award: 2013[536]
  • Eliason Merit Award: 2018[537]
  • Malmö Walk of Fame: 2012[538]
  • Medal of the City of Malmö: 2015[539]
  • Swedish Newcomer of the Year: 2001[540]
  • Swedish Football Personality of the Year: 2002[541]
  • Swedish Forward of the Year: 2005, 2007, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020[541]
  • Swedish Male Athlete of the Year: 2008, 2010, 2013, 2015[542][543][544][545]
  • Swedish Goal of the Year: 2012, 2013[541]
Records
Overall
  • The only player to have scored at least a goal in De Klassieker in Netherlands, Derby della Madonnina and Derby d'Italia in Italy, El Clásico and Derbi barceloní in Spain, Le Classique in France, Manchester Derby and North-West Derby in England, El Tráfico and California Clásico in United States.[360]
  • The only player to score in the UEFA Champions League with six teams: Ajax, Juventus, Inter Milan, Barcelona, AC Milan and Paris Saint-Germain.[546]
  • The only player to score on his Premier League, Serie A, La Liga, Ligue 1 and UEFA Champions League debut.[547]
  • The only player to have scored 50 Serie A goals for both Inter Milan and AC Milan.[548]
  • The only foreign player to have won Capocannoniere with two teams, in addition to two teams from the same city: Inter Milan (2008–09) and AC Milan (2011–12).[549]
  • One of three players, after Adrian Mutu and John Carew, to have scored in European competitions with seven different teams.[550]
  • Oldest player to score in Serie A history.[240]
Paris Saint-Germain
  • First Paris Saint-Germain player to be top goalscorer in three Ligue 1 seasons (2012–13, 2013–14, 2015–16)[137] (equalled by Kylian Mbappé).
  • Most Ligue 1 goals in a single season: 38 in 2015–16 season[551][552]
  • Fastest Ligue 1 hat-trick: 9 minutes (vs. Troyes, 13 March 2016)[553]
  • Longest goalscoring run in Ligue 1: 9 consecutive matches in 2015–16 season (shared with Vahid Halilhodžić)[137]
  • Most goals scored in a single season (all competitions): 50 in 2015–16 season
  • All-time top scorer in Le Classique: 11 goals[554]
Manchester United
  • The second player (after Ian Storey-Moore in the 1971–72 season[a]) to score in his first three league matches[547]
  • The second player (after Javier Hernández in the 2010–11 season) to score in the Premier League, FA Cup, League Cup, European competition and Community Shield in a single season (2016–17 season)[547]
  • Scored the 25,000th goal in Premier League history[555]
  • The oldest player to reach 15 goals in a single Premier League season (2016–17 season at 35 years and 125 days; since eclipsed by Cristiano Ronaldo in 2022)[556]
LA Galaxy
  • Most MLS regular season goals in a season: 30 in 2019 season[557]
Sweden
  • All-time top goalscorer for Sweden national team: 62 goals[558]
  • Sweden's top goalscorer in UEFA European Championship: 6 goals[559]
  • Sweden's only player to score in three consecutive UEFA European Championship tournaments: 2004, 2008 and 2012[560]
  • Most Guldbollen (Swedish Male footballer of the Year) awards: 12[561]

Books

  • Ibrahimovic, Zlatan; David Lagercrantz (22 March 2012). Jag är Zlatan Ibrahimović: min historia [I Am Zlatan Ibrahimović] (in Swedish). Stockholm: Albert Bonniers Förlag. ISBN 978-0-241-96683-9. OCLC 857923830. UK edition: ISBN 978-0-241-96683-9.
  • Ibrahimovic, Zlatan; Luigi Garlando (2 December 2021). Adrenalina. My untold stories [Adrenaline: My Untold Stories] (in Italian). Milan: Cairo. ISBN 9788830901988. OCLC 1360352275.
